content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 25 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folio 222 | **Source Images:** source-texts/hebrew-OT/DEU_025_page_001.jpg | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), p. 158 — DEU_025_vaticanus_page_001.jpg

---

**1** When there is a dispute between men, and they draw near to the judgment, and the judges judge them — they shall declare the righteous one righteous and condemn the wicked one.

**2** And it shall be, if the wicked one is deserving of beating, the judge shall cause him to fall down and to be beaten before his face, according to his wickedness, by number.

**3** Forty [stripes] he may beat him — he shall not add; lest he add to beat him beyond these [with] many blows, and your brother be dishonored in your eyes.

**4** You shall not muzzle an ox while it is threshing.

**5** When brothers dwell together and one of them dies and he has no son, the wife of the dead one shall not be outside to a stranger-man; her brother-in-law shall come in to her and take her to himself as a wife and perform the duty of a brother-in-law [yibbum] toward her.

**6** And it shall be: the firstborn whom she bears shall succeed in the name of his dead brother, that his name may not be blotted out from Yisra'el.

**7** But if the man does not desire to take his sister-in-law, then his sister-in-law shall go up to the gate, to the elders, and say: My brother-in-law refuses to raise up for his brother a name in Yisra'el — he has not been willing to perform the duty of a brother-in-law for me.

**8** Then the elders of his city shall call to him and speak to him; and he shall stand and say: I do not desire to take her.

**9** And his sister-in-law shall draw near to him before the eyes of the elders, and remove his sandal from off his foot, and spit in his face [chalitzah]; and she shall answer and say: So shall it be done to the man who does not build up the house of his brother.

**10** And his name shall be called in Yisra'el: the house of the one whose sandal was pulled off.

**11** When men fight together, a man and his brother, and the wife of the one draws near to rescue her husband from the hand of the one who strikes him, and she stretches out her hand and seizes him by his genitals —

**12** then you shall cut off her hand; your eye shall not show pity.

**13** You shall not have in your bag differing stones [for weights] — a large and a small.

**14** You shall not have in your house differing measures [ephah] — a large and a small.

**15** A whole and just stone [weight] you shall have; a whole and just ephah you shall have — so that your days may be long upon the land that the LORD your God is giving you.

**16** For an abomination to the LORD your God is everyone who does these things, everyone who acts unjustly.

**17** Remember what Amalek did to you on the road when you were coming out of Mitsrayim —

**18** how he met you on the road and struck down all the stragglers at your rear when you were faint and weary; and he did not fear God.

**19** And it shall be, when the LORD your God gives you rest from all your enemies all around, in the land that the LORD your God is giving you as an inheritance to possess it, you shall blot out the memory of Amalek from under the heavens [timcheh et-zecher Amalek] — you shall not forget.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

**V.4 — Ox muzzling:** Hebrew lo-tachsom shor be-disho. A stand-alone aphorism in the MT. The LXX Vaticanus (p. 158) renders: ου φιμωσεις βουν αλοωντα — precise one-for-one. Paul quotes this in 1 Cor 9:9 and 1 Tim 5:18.

**V.5 — Yibbum (levirate marriage):** The term yibbum derives from yavam (brother-in-law). EMET preserves "duty of a brother-in-law" rather than flattening to "marry her." The LXX reads: επιγαμβρευσεται αυτην — "shall take her as a kinswoman-wife."

**V.9 — Chalitzah:** The sandal-removal ceremony. EMET preserves the literal sequence: approach → remove sandal → spit in face → speak the formula. The LXX: αφελει το υποδημα — "shall remove the sandal."

**V.11–12 — Protective violence:** The severe penalty (hand-cutting) for seizing a man's genitals in a brawl. LXX reads συλλαβηται την χειρα αυτου επι τοις αιδοιοις αυτου — "seizes his private parts." The MT is graphic; EMET renders literally.

**V.13 — Differing stones:** eben va-aven (lit. "stone and stone") — the idiom for dishonest weights. LXX: λιθον και λιθον — identical.

**V.17–19 — Amalek command:** Timcheh et-zecher Amalek — "you shall blot out the memory of Amalek." The imperative is singular (addressed to all Yisra'el corporately). The LXX (p. 158): εξαλειψεις το μνημοσυνον Αμαληκ — "you shall obliterate the memorial of Amalek." The final clause lo tishkach ("you shall not forget") is grammatically paradoxical: the command to blot out requires remembering to do so. LXX confirms this reading.

— EMET
